countries/el-salvador.summary: CRISTOSAL SHUTDOWN + CONSTITUTIONAL AMENDMENT (ongoing Jul 5): Cristosal — El Salvador's leading human rights organization — suspended operations citing "escalating repression"; Ruth López remains imprisoned. Legislative Assembly amended constitution allowing indefinite presidential re-election. Foreign Agents Law (May 2026): 140+ rights defenders/journalists fled; 3+ organizations shut down including APES. 86+ political prisoners; 245+ facing persecution. State of exception Year 5. Life sentences for persons as young as 12. Downgrade proposal (20.3 to 15.0) REMAINS PENDING — not yet applied. NBC News: political exiles say "history is repeating itself."

countries/palestine.summary: CEASEFIRE VIOLATIONS ONGOING (Jul 5): Israel killed 3 Palestinians in Gaza Jul 1 despite ceasefire. Al Jazeera documents 3,465 Israeli violations of ceasefire since Oct 10, 2025; 1,053 Palestinians killed since truce. Aid blockage continues: only 36% of allocated trucks entered Gaza (55,539 of 156,000 Oct-Jun). Israel controls ~64% of Gaza territory. 72,945+ Palestinians killed since Oct 2023. UNRWA Situation Report 217: ongoing humanitarian nightmare. UN Security Council July 2026 agenda includes Palestine question. ICJ proceedings ongoing.

countries/nigeria.summary: LEAN SEASON PEAK — 35M+ FOOD INSECURE (continuing Jul 5): 35M acutely food insecure nationally — worst in a decade. 15,000+ at IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe in Borno. 3M children under five facing life-threatening severe acute malnutrition. ~5.5M without lifesaving food aid (WFP can reach only 740,000 of 6.2M in need). 150 WFP nutrition clinics in Borno/Yobe shut down — 300,000 children at risk of wasting. Communities reporting people joining armed groups for food. WFP needs $129M by July. FAO: West Africa 52.8M facing acute food insecurity June-August.

countries/china.summary: ETHNIC UNITY LAW IN FORCE (Jul 1) — TRANSNATIONAL REPRESSION CONFIRMED: Law enacted July 1; senior officials confirmed extraterritorial applicability at Jun 24 State Council press conference — overseas individuals and organizations can be held liable. Al Jazeera Jul 2: "law extends its legal reach overseas." Al Jazeera Jul 3: Inside Story on forced assimilation. Taiwan government issued formal response amid fears of cross-border repression. Amnesty: "legal blueprint for cultural erasure" for Uyghurs, Tibetans, Mongolians. Germany Foreign Ministry: "great concern." European Parliament April 30 resolution condemning law.

countries/lebanon.summary: IDF STRIKES — "NO CEASEFIRE" IN SOUTH (Jul 5 continuing): IDF struck ~10 Hezbollah sites in southern Lebanon in early July (Bint Jbeil, Beit Yahoun, Kounine, Baraashit). Lebanese President Aoun condemned "continuing violations." Multiple killed and wounded daily despite the April 16 US-brokered truce framework. 4,000+ killed since March 2 resumption; 1M+ displaced (>20% of population). 76% of south Lebanon farmers displaced; 22% of agricultural land damaged. 1M+ facing food insecurity. Lebanese military documented violations in Khiam, Bint Jbeil, Dibbin.

countries/venezuela.summary: EARTHQUAKE DAY 11 (Jul 5): Official death toll 2,954 — up 309 from Jul 4. ~50,000 still unaccounted; forensic pathologists warn true figure may be three times higher. UN ODRR: $37B direct damage; WHO DG spotlights Venezuela as a global health emergency (Jul 3). 16,000+ homeless; 58,870 buildings damaged/destroyed. WFP appealed for $50M to feed 500,000 for three months. UNICEF supplies for 100,000 in. Monsoon season risk mounting. Venezuelan government $200M reconstruction fund covers <1% of estimated need.

countries/ukraine.summary: ONGOING STRIKES (Jul 5) — JULY 2026 RECORD CIVILIAN TOLL: Russia struck Dnipro, Poltava, Zaporizhzhia Jul 4 (1 killed). July 1-3 Kyiv attack: 30+ killed including 9-floor residential building collapse, Darnytskyi district. July 2026 total: 286 killed / 1,388 injured — ~20% above same period 2025, highest monthly rate since 2023. 62,716+ civilian casualties since Feb 2022. EU Special Tribunal for crime of aggression now operational. EU sanctioned 9 Bucha massacre perpetrators. UK-brokered minerals deal signed (not scored — diplomatic).

countries/mali.summary: JNIM BAMAKO SIEGE (ongoing Jul 5) + HRW GRAVE ABUSES REPORT (Jun 28): JNIM siege blocking all major supply roads since April 28. Severe shortages of fuel, food, medicine in Bamako. HRW Jun 28 (in window): "grave abuses amid renewed fighting" documenting civilian executions. Bus landmine Jun 1 (8 killed, 42 injured). FAO/WFP Joint briefing on Sahel food crisis Jul 9 (upcoming). 5M in humanitarian need; 1.56M acutely food insecure; response only 18.5% funded. 52.8M facing food insecurity in West Africa/Sahel lean season.

countries/south-sudan.summary: IPC PHASE 5 IN 4 COUNTIES — FAMINE RISK (ongoing Jul 5): 7.8M at IPC Phase 3+ (56% of population). 73,300 at IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe — 160% increase from prior estimate — in Akobo, Fangak, Nyirol, Uror (Jonglei) and Luakpiny/Nasir, Ulang (Upper Nile). Credible famine risk in four counties. 300,000 displaced in Jonglei; 200,000 displaced from Akobo since March 6. 2.2M children acutely malnourished; 700,000 children projected severe acute malnutrition through July. Cholera, malaria, measles outbreaks compounding. DRC Ebola spreading to border province (Haut-Uele).

countries/haiti.summary: SCOTUS TPS RULING (Jun 25, in window) — 350K HAITIANS AT RISK + GANG CRISIS: Supreme Court 6-3 ruling allows termination of TPS for 350,000 Haitians; deportees face kidnapping/extortion risks as gangs control ~90% of Port-au-Prince. 1.5M+ internally displaced; 5.8M (52%) at crisis food insecurity; 1.8M at emergency. District judge separately ruled DHS decision unlawful — legal uncertainty ongoing. Haitian Times Jul 2: communities reeling. 8,200+ killed Jan 2025-Mar 2026. WLRN Jul 2: "humanitarian crisis looming." TPS holders contribute $2.6B annually to Florida economy.

countries/france.summary: WESTERN-EUROPE BATCH: France reported one imported Bundibugyo Ebola case from DRC (within window). Case managed under isolation protocols. ECDC considers EU/EEA infection likelihood "very low."

countries/democratic-republic-of-c.summary: EBOLA BUNDIBUGYO — 1,406 CONFIRMED, 438 DEAD (Jul 3 update): As of July 3, 1,406 confirmed cases with 438 deaths — average 38 new confirmed daily. Active spread: Ituri (1,333 cases), North Kivu, South Kivu, Haut-Uele (borders South Sudan). International: France (1 imported, within window), Uganda (20 confirmed, no new cases since Jun 21 — at window boundary). WHO DG spotlights DRC outbreak as global emergency Jul 3. No approved vaccine or treatment for Bundibugyo strain. PHEIC active. M23/FARDC conflict ongoing; 7.2M internally displaced. UN Security Council July agenda includes DRC.

countries/iran.summary: KHAMENEI FUNERAL DAY 2 (Jul 5) — MOJTABA STILL ABSENT; TALKS PAUSED UNTIL JUL 11: Three of Khamenei's sons joined mass mourning July 5 but Mojtaba Khamenei — new Supreme Leader — remained absent, reportedly in hiding due to security threats. Trump granted Iran a "week off" for funeral; US-Iran nuclear and Hormuz talks paused, scheduled to resume July 11. Iran NCRI July 5 news brief: ongoing executions; political prisoner Arghavan Fallahi sentenced to death July 1. Execution surge: 784+ YTD (37-year high). 30+ political prisoners hanged since March 19 under cover of war. Amnesty May 2026: mass arbitrary arrests and political executions.

countries/russia.summary: KYIV ATTACKS (Jul 1-5) + ACCOUNTABILITY MEASURES: Russia struck Kyiv Jul 1-3 (30+ killed, 9-floor residential building collapsed; 74 missiles + 496 drones). Jul 4: Dnipro, Poltava, Zaporizhzhia strikes (1 killed). July 2026 civilian toll: 286/1,388 — highest monthly rate since 2023. EU Council sanctioned 9 Bucha massacre individuals (crimes against humanity). EU Special Tribunal for crime of aggression against Ukraine now operational. EU sanctions on Russia renewed to July 2027. 20,500+ Ukrainian children forcibly deported.

countries/sudan.summary: UN "RED ALERT" (Jul 3) — RSF EL OBEID SIEGE TIGHTENING: UN Human Rights Chief Türk issued "red alert" over impending atrocities in El Obeid (North Kordofan). HRW Jul 3: RSF drone strikes hit 16+ civilian targets including hospitals, schools, power stations; 45 civilians killed in 15 drone attacks Jun 6-28 alone; food prices up 300%; 500,000 + 105,000 IDPs at siege risk. UNHCR relief truck (50MT) destroyed in drone strike Tendelti Jul 1. Amnesty Jul 2026: crimes against humanity at El Fasher confirmed. Sudan more people in famine than rest of world combined. Red Cross stepping up El Obeid response.

fortune-500/unitedhealth-group.summary: DOJ CRIMINAL + CIVIL PROBE — Q2 EARNINGS JULY 29: DOJ criminal probe extends to Optum Rx and physician reimbursement arrangements (within window context). Civil probe: inflated diagnoses for higher Medicare Advantage payments. Senate Grassley investigation confirms "aggressively gaming" Medicare Advantage. Third-party review in progress (complete by end Q3). Q2 earnings call July 29 — potential bellwether. DOJ Jun 23 national healthcare fraud takedown ($6.5B, 455 defendants) maintains regulatory pressure. Medicare Advantage kickback lawsuits moving forward.

countries/pakistan.summary: AFGHANISTAN CONFLICT ESCALATION (Jul 1 in window): Taliban launched drone strikes into Pakistan's Balochistan July 1 — first aerial retaliation. Cumulative: 372 Afghan civilians killed Q1 2026. 3 Pakistani rangers killed Karachi Jun 27. Relief web Jun 2026: food security under pressure from conflict.
